goal: Build Route 9 + Route 10 + Rock Tunnel + Route 11 + Diglett's Cave — connecting routes and dungeons
abstract_tasks: Implement Route 9 map with 6 trainers (Camper Dean, Picnicker Heidi, Camper Sid, Picnicker Edna, Hiker Tim, Hiker Sidney) and hidden Ether at (41,15)
Implement Route 10 split (north: Power Plant entrance; south: Hiker Jim + Pokefan M Robert) with Voltorb/Electabuzz wild encounters
Implement Rock Tunnel two-floor dungeon (1F + B1F) with wild encounters including Cubone, Geodude, Machop, Marowak, Kangaskhan (B1F rare), Haunter (night)
Implement Rock Tunnel item pickups: Elixer, TM Steel Wing (1F itemballs); hidden X Accuracy at (24,4), X Defend at (21,15), Max Potion at B1F (4,14)
Implement Route 11 with 4 trainers (Youngster Owen/Jason, Psychic Herman/Fidel), Hoppip/Magnemite wild encounters, hidden Revive at (32,5)
Implement Diglett's Cave shortcut (Route 2 entrance at (12,7) <-> Vermilion City at (34,7)), Diglett Lv2-32 + Dugtrio wild encounters, hidden Max Revive at (6,11)

source_data.kanto_locations.md:
### Route 9
- Connections: Cerulean City (west) to Rock Tunnel entrance (east)
- Wild Pokemon: Rattata Lv15, Spearow Lv15, Raticate Lv15, Fearow Lv15, Marowak Lv18, Venonat/Venomoth/Zubat (night)

### Route 10
- Connections: Lavender Town (south) to Rock Tunnel (north); Power Plant
- Wild Pokemon: Spearow Lv15, Voltorb Lv17, Raticate Lv15, Fearow Lv15, Marowak Lv15, Electabuzz Lv16-18, Venonat/Venomoth (night)
- Key Location: Power Plant (must restore Machine Part; Zapdos NOT present)

### Route 11
- Connections: Vermilion City (west) to Route 12 (east)
- Wild Pokemon: Hoppip Lv14-16, Raticate Lv13, Magnemite Lv15, Pidgeotto Lv16, Rattata Lv16, Drowzee/Meowth/Hypno/Noctowl (night)

### Diglett's Cave
- Location: Between Route 2 and Route 11/Vermilion
- Wild Pokemon: Diglett Lv2-24 (morn/day), Dugtrio Lv16-32
- Notes: Unchanged from Gen 1; important shortcut

### Rock Tunnel
- Location: Route 9 to Route 10
- Floors: 2 (1F, B1F)
- Wild Pokemon 1F: Cubone Lv10, Geodude Lv11, Machop Lv12, Zubat Lv12, Machoke Lv15, Marowak Lv12; Night: Haunter Lv17
- Wild Pokemon B1F: Cubone Lv12, Geodude Lv14, Onix Lv16, Zubat Lv12, Marowak Lv15, Kangaskhan Lv15; Night: Haunter Lv15, Golbat Lv15
source_data.kanto_map_scripts.md:
### Route 9 (Cerulean -> Rock Tunnel / Power Plant)
Trainers: Camper Dean, Picnicker Heidi, Camper Sid, Picnicker Edna, Hiker Tim, Hiker Sidney (6 trainers)
Hidden Items: Ether at (41, 15)

### Route 10 (Rock Tunnel -> Lavender area)
Route 10 South Trainers: Hiker Jim, Pokefan M Robert (2 trainers)
Route 10 North — Power Plant entrance

### Route 11 (Vermilion -> east)
Trainers: Youngster Owen, Youngster Jason, Psychic Herman, Psychic Fidel (4 trainers)
Items: Fruit Tree (FRUITTREE_ROUTE_11)
Hidden Items: Revive at (32, 5)

### Rock Tunnel (1F + B1F)
Warps: Route 9 <-> Route 10 South, internal connections (6 warps on 1F)
Items (1F): Elixer (itemball), TM Steel Wing (itemball)
Hidden Items (1F): X Accuracy at (24, 4), X Defend at (21, 15)
Hidden Items (B1F): Max Potion at (4, 14)
No trainers in cave itself.

### Diglett's Cave
Warps: Vermilion City <-> Route 2
NPCs: Pokefan M — 'A bunch of DIGLETT popped out of the ground!'
Hidden Items: Max Revive at (6, 11)
source_data.warp_connections.md:
### Diglett's Cave
- Route 2 entrance (12,7) <-> Vermilion City entrance (34,7)

### Rock Tunnel
- Route 9 entrance (48,15) -> 1F
- 1F <-> B1F (multiple staircases)
- Exit to Route 10 side

| Route | Warps To |
|Route 9 | Rock Tunnel 1F (48,15) |

unstructured_notes: Rock Tunnel has no trainers inside — only on the flanking routes. Kangaskhan in B1F is a notable rare find. Diglett's Cave is a critical shortcut that saves significant travel time between Pewter and Vermilion areas. Route 10 is split into north (Power Plant area) and south (Rock Tunnel exit); the split is important for Power Plant sidequest access. Steel Wing TM in Rock Tunnel 1F is valuable. Electabuzz on Route 10 is a rare high-level encounter for this point of the game.

goal: QA audit of sprints 145-146 — verify Pallet/Cinnabar/Seafoam, Rock Tunnel, Diglett's Cave connectivity
abstract_tasks: Verify Pallet Town warps (Red's House, Blue's House, Oak's Lab) and Oak event flags (EVENT_TALKED_TO_OAK_IN_KANTO, EVENT_OPENED_MT_SILVER) fire correctly with 16-badge check
Verify Cinnabar Island destroyed state: only Pokecenter exists, Blue appears at (EVENT_BLUE_IN_CINNABAR), dialogue leads player to Viridian Gym
Verify Seafoam Islands: Blaine's gym accessible via Route 20 warp at (38,7), no gym trainers, Volcano Badge awarded
Verify Rock Tunnel two-floor connectivity: Route 9 entrance (48,15) -> 1F, multiple 1F<->B1F staircases, exit to Route 10 south
Verify Diglett's Cave bidirectional warp: Route 2 (12,7) <-> Vermilion (34,7), Diglett/Dugtrio wild encounter table active
Verify Route 9 trainer sight ranges, Route 10 Power Plant entrance tile accessible, Route 11 Fruit Tree respawn mechanics

source_data.kanto_map_scripts.md:
### Cinnabar Island
NPCs: Blue — appears only when EVENT_BLUE_IN_CINNABAR is set. Long dialogue about volcano, then tells player to come to Viridian Gym. Teleport animation to leave. Clears EVENT_VIRIDIAN_GYM_BLUE (makes Blue + Gym Guide appear in Viridian Gym).
Hidden Items: Rare Candy at (9, 1)
Signs: Cinnabar Gym (notice: relocated to Seafoam Islands — BLAINE)
Flypoint: ENGINE_FLYPOINT_CINNABAR

### Seafoam Gym (Blaine's relocated gym)
Leader: Blaine (VOLCANOBADGE)
Badge flag: ENGINE_VOLCANOBADGE
Trainer: loadtrainer BLAINE, BLAINE1
Event: EVENT_BEAT_BLAINE
No gym trainers — just Blaine in a cave
Gym Guide appears after battle (EVENT_SEAFOAM_GYM_GYM_GUIDE)
Location: Inside Seafoam Islands cave, accessed from Route 20
Blaine's Intro: 'Waaah! My GYM in CINNABAR burned down. My fire-breathing Pokemon and I are homeless because of the volcano.'

### Diglett's Cave
Warps: Vermilion City <-> Route 2 (with internal connections)
NPCs: Pokefan M — 'A bunch of DIGLETT popped out of the ground! That was shocking.'
Hidden Items: Max Revive at (6, 11)
source_data.kanto_locations.md:
### Pallet Town
- Connections: North to Route 1; South to Route 21 (Surf)
- Buildings: Player Red's House, Professor Oak's Lab, Rival Blue's House
- Key NPCs: Professor Oak (evaluates Pokedex), Daisy Oak (grooms Pokemon for happiness; daily)

### Cinnabar Island
- Connections: North to Route 21 (Surf); East to Route 20 (Surf)
- Buildings: Pokemon Center only
- Gym: Blaine relocated to Seafoam Islands
- Notes: The island was devastated by a volcanic eruption. Only the Pokemon Center remains.
- Flypoint: Yes

### Seafoam Islands
- Location: Route 20
- Gym: Blaine — Magcargo Lv45, Magmar Lv45, Rapidash Lv50 — Volcano Badge.
- Wild Pokemon: Zubat, Golbat, Seel, Dewgong, Psyduck, Golduck, Slowpoke, Slowbro, Jynx
source_data.game_progression.md:
### Red Battle
- After obtaining all 16 badges, Mt. Silver entrance opens (Route 28)
- Professor Oak in Pallet grants access when 16 badges checked
- EVENT_OPENED_MT_SILVER set; unlocks Route 28 -> Mt. Silver

unstructured_notes: Key QA focus: Blue's event chain must be correct — EVENT_BLUE_IN_CINNABAR set when player first arrives, Blue leaves Cinnabar and triggers EVENT_VIRIDIAN_GYM_BLUE. Seafoam Gym is accessed via Route 20 route warp, NOT through a town center — test that the warp destination is correct. Oak's Mt. Silver unlock checks all 16 badge flags individually. Diglett's Cave is a simple two-entrance linear cave; confirm neither entrance loops back to itself.

goal: Build Mt. Silver — exterior, interior floors, summit (Red battle location), high-level wild encounters
abstract_tasks: Implement Silver Cave Outside with Pokemon Center warp, Room 1 warp, hidden Full Restore at (9,25), and FLYPOINT_SILVER_CAVE
Implement Silver Cave Room 1 with itemballs (Max Elixer, Protein, Escape Rope, Ultra Ball), hidden items (Dire Hit at (16,23), Ultra Ball at (17,12)), connection to Room 2
Implement Silver Cave Room 2 with itemballs (Calcium, Ultra Ball, PP Up), hidden Max Potion at (14,31), connections to Room 1, Room 3, and two Item Rooms
Implement Silver Cave Room 3 (Summit) with Trainer Red static encounter: object_event for SPRITE_RED, silent ellipsis dialogue, loadtrainer RED/RED1, post-battle HealParty + credits
Implement Silver Cave Item Rooms with Parasect/Golbat wild encounters and hidden items

Implement all wild encounter tables for Mt. Silver rooms: Room 1 (Larvitar Lv20, Misdreavus Lv45, Onix, Graveler, Golbat); Room 2 (Golbat Lv46-48, Parasect Lv46-48); Room 3 (Golbat Lv51, Onix Lv48, Ursaring Lv50, Larvitar Lv15-20, Pupitar Lv20); night variants

### Silver Cave Room 1
Warps: Silver Cave Outside <-> Silver Cave Room 2
Items: Max Elixer (itemball), Protein (itemball), Escape Rope (itemball), Ultra Ball (itemball)
Hidden Items: Dire Hit at (16, 23), Ultra Ball at (17, 12)

### Silver Cave Room 2
Warps: Silver Cave Room 1 <-> Silver Cave Room 3, Silver Cave Item Rooms (x2)
Items: Calcium (itemball), Ultra Ball (itemball), PP Up (itemball)
Hidden Items: Max Potion at (14, 31)

### Silver Cave Room 3 (Summit)
Warps: Silver Cave Room 2
The Final Battle: Trainer Red
object_event 9, 10, SPRITE_RED — controlled by EVENT_RED_IN_MT_SILVER
Music fades out (special FadeOutMusic)
Red's dialogue: '......' (silent — just ellipses)
Battle: loadtrainer RED, RED1
Win/loss text: '...'
After battle: Red disappears, screen fades to black, party healed (special HealParty), credits roll
Red's Team (hardcoded): Pikachu Lv81, Espeon Lv73, Snorlax Lv75, Venusaur Lv77, Charizard Lv77, Blastoise Lv77

source_data.special_encounters.md:
### Red Battle
After obtaining all 16 badges, Mt. Silver entrance opens (Route 28)
Climb to summit to battle Trainer Red
Red's team: Pikachu Lv81, Espeon Lv73, Snorlax Lv75, Venusaur Lv77, Charizard Lv77, Blastoise Lv77
The final challenge of the game; no dialogue, just battle
source_data.kanto_locations.md:
### Route 28
- Connections: Route to Mt. Silver (post-game, all 16 badges)
- Wild Pokemon: Tangela Lv39, Ponyta Lv40, Rapidash Lv40, Arbok Lv42, Doduo Lv41, Dodrio Lv43, Poliwhirl/Golbat (night)

### Indigo Plateau
- Notes: Same Elite Four as Johto storyline; rematches have higher levels

unstructured_notes: Red's battle is the true final boss. The implementation must have: (1) access gated by EVENT_OPENED_MT_SILVER (all 16 badges + Oak visit), (2) music fade-out before encounter, (3) Red's '...' dialogue with no name, (4) post-battle party heal and second credit roll. Larvitar in the upper rooms is significant — it's one of the few places to catch Larvitar in the wild. Pupitar also appears. Mt. Silver rooms do not have a strict linear order — Room 2 branches to Item Rooms. Misdreavus only appears at night in the upper caves.

source_data.wild_encounters.md: Crystal version-exclusive notes: No Mankey/Primeape (Route 42 Gold only), no Growlithe (Routes 35-37 Gold only), no Spinarak (night Route 2 Crystal still has it), no Teddiursa/Ursaring in Kanto routes (Silver only). Electabuzz on Route 10 is Crystal-only among routes. Slugma on Routes 16-18 is Crystal-specific.
source_data.special_encounters.md:
### Crystal-Only Content
- New Pokemon availability changes (e.g., Phanpy/Teddiursa availability differs)
### Gold-Only Pokemon (not in Crystal wild)
- Mankey, Primeape (Route 42)
- Growlithe (Route 35, 36, 37)
- Spinarak, Ariados (Route 2 night in Crystal actually keeps Ariados)
- Teddiursa, Ursaring (Route 45, Mt. Silver — different per version)
### Silver-Only Pokemon
- Vulpix, Ninetales (Route 36, 37)
- Meowth, Persian (Route 39)
- Phanpy, Donphan (Route 45, Mt. Silver)
- Skarmory (Route 45)

unstructured_notes: Kanto wild encounters use the same morning/day/night system as Johto. Most Kanto routes have Gen 2 Pokemon added (Snubbull, Slugma, Houndour on night routes) that weren't in Gen 1. Crystal has unique wild availability vs Gold/Silver — use pokecrystal ASM as the definitive source. Key rare encounters: Pikachu on Route 2, Chansey on Routes 13-14, Mr. Mime on Route 21, Electabuzz on Route 10 (day). Cerulean Cave has Ditto and Electrode at decent rates. Silver Cave upper floors have Larvitar which is only available in Mt. Silver in Crystal.

source_data.game_progression.md:
152. After obtaining all 16 badges, Mt. Silver entrance opens (Route 28)
153. Multi-floor cave dungeon with high-level wild Pokemon (Larvitar, Ursaring, Sneasel, Misdreavus, Pupitar)
154. Summit of Mt. Silver
155. Red says nothing — '...' — and battle begins
156. Red's Team: Pikachu Lv81, Espeon Lv73, Snorlax Lv75, Venusaur Lv77, Charizard Lv77, Blastoise Lv77
157. Highest-level trainer in the game
158. Defeating Red triggers credits again — the true ending

unstructured_notes: Key QA: Red must NOT appear before all 16 badges are collected and Oak is visited. If game is in post-game state with only 15 badges, Red should be absent. Post-battle HealParty is a special routine — verify it heals all party members to full HP and restores PP before credits. Cerulean Cave is notable: Mewtwo does NOT appear (Gen 1 only) but Ditto, Electrode, and Misdreavus (night) do. Ensure no Mewtwo encounter data was accidentally included.

source_data.gym_leader_rematches.md:
In Pokemon Crystal, gym leaders do NOT have rematches.
Rematches are available from registered Phone Trainers.
Phone Trainers (Rematchable) include trainers from National Park, Olivine Lighthouse, Routes 26-39.

unstructured_notes: Important: Kanto gym leaders do NOT have rematch versions in Crystal — rematches are from phone trainers only. Lt. Surge's actual team uses Raichu (not just Electrode) as his primary, which is different from the location reference list — use the all_trainers.md data as authoritative. Blue's team at Lv56-58 is significantly stronger than all other gym leaders. Sabrina's teleporter puzzle uses 32 warp events internally. Janine's gym has a disguise gimmick where 4 trainers look like Janine before revealing themselves. Fuchsia Gym auto-defeats 4 trainers when leader beaten (Alice, Linda, Cindy, Barry).

source_data.game_progression.md:
141. Professor Elm gives S.S. Ticket
142. Board S.S. Aqua at Olivine City port -> arrives in Vermilion City
143. Alternatively: complete Copycat sidequest for Magnet Train Pass (Goldenrod <-> Saffron)

### S.S. Aqua — Travel to Kanto
- Route: Olivine Port -> Vermilion Port
- Events on ship: Various trainer battles, meet rival's Pokeball (in a cabin)

unstructured_notes: S.S. Aqua is the primary method of traveling from Johto to Kanto post-game. The ship has multiple floors/decks and various cabins. A notable story beat involves a locked cabin where Silver's rival events are referenced. The sailor NPC who gave HM04 Strength in Olivine Cafe is separate from the ship crew. Day-of-week schedule is important: Wednesday and Sunday from Olivine, Monday and Friday from Vermilion — missing these days means waiting. Magnet Train is the alternative fast travel (Saffron<->Goldenrod) but requires Copycat's Lost Item quest first. S.S. Ticket from Elm is required to board.

source_data.kanto_map_scripts.md:
### Saffron Magnet Train Station
Connects Saffron <-> Goldenrod. Requires Pass (from Copycat quest) and EVENT_RESTORED_POWER_TO_KANTO.

### Vermilion Gym
Gym Puzzle: 15 trash can bg_events (3x5 grid). In Gen 2 Crystal, traps are not active (Gym Guide confirms: 'the traps aren't active right now').

### Fuchsia Gym
Gym Gimmick: All 4 gym trainers disguised as Janine (using SPRITE_JANINE / variable sprites). Each spins when interacted with, then reveals true identity:
- SPRITE_FUCHSIA_GYM_1 -> Lass Alice
- SPRITE_FUCHSIA_GYM_2 -> Lass Linda
- SPRITE_FUCHSIA_GYM_3 -> Picnicker Cindy
- SPRITE_FUCHSIA_GYM_4 -> Camper Barry
After each battle, sprite changes via variablesprite to show real identity.

Kanto Sidequests:
- Copycat's Doll: Get Lost Item from Vermilion Fan Club -> return to Copycat in Saffron -> receive Magnet Train Pass

unstructured_notes: Critical QA: Surge's trash can puzzle is INACTIVE in Crystal (unlike Gen 1) — the Gym Guide explicitly says so. Do not implement an active trash can mechanic. Blue's gym has no gym trainers at all — just Blue and the Gym Guide. Misty requires Power Plant quest completion before she appears in her gym. Brock's Gym has only 1 trainer (Camper Jerry). The Magnet Train requires two conditions: PASS item AND power restoration — both must be checked.

## 13. Ecruteak City

## 16. Goldenrod Radio Tower Crisis
### Goldenrod Underground — Rival Battle 4
Trigger: Encounter Silver in Underground during Radio Tower crisis

## 20. Victory Road
### Victory Road — Rival Battle 5
Trigger: Encounter Silver in Victory Road
Battle: RIVAL1_5 — Full team of 6 Pokemon (~Lv34-38)
Dialogue: Silver beginning to question his approach to Pokemon

### Mt. Moon Square — SCENE_MOUNTMOON_RIVAL_BATTLE
Rival appears, walks toward player
Battle: loadtrainer RIVAL2, RIVAL2_1_TOTODILE/CHIKORITA/CYNDAQUIL
After: EVENT_BEAT_RIVAL_IN_MT_MOON

unstructured_notes: The rival (Silver) has one of the most complex trainer data sets in the game: 3 variants per encounter (one per player starter), 8 encounter locations. His team always uses the type-advantage starter vs the player. Recurring team members: Gastly->Haunter, Zubat->Golbat, Magnemite->Magneton, and eventually Sneasel and Kadabra join. The Kanto Mt. Moon battle uses RIVAL2 trainer data (a separate set from RIVAL1) with a fully evolved team around Lv40. Silver's dialogue arc across the battles is important for characterization — early: arrogant thief, Victory Road: beginning to doubt himself.

source_data.elite_four_strategies.md:
Pre-E4 Preparation: Recommended Level Range 42-50
Will: Difficulty 3/10 — Dark-type moves hit everything super-effectively
Koga: Difficulty 4/10 — Koga is annoying, not hard; bring Full Heals for status
Koga Forretress: Only weak to Fire (4x). Beware Explosion.
Koga Muk: Minimize makes it nearly unhittable. Use Swift.
Bruno: Difficulty 3/10 — Psychic or Flying sweeps most of team
Bruno Hitmontop: Beware Counter — use special attacks
Bruno Machamp at Lv46: Foresight removes Ghost immunity
Karen: Houndoom Lv47 is dangerous; Gengar with Destiny Bond is a trap
Lance: 3 Dragonite require Ice Beam/Blizzard. No badge boost for champion
source_data.story_events_by_map.md:
## 21. Indigo Plateau — Elite Four + Champion
1. Will (Psychic): Xatu Lv40, Jynx Lv41, Exeggutor Lv41, Slowbro Lv41, Xatu Lv42
2. Koga (Poison): Ariados Lv40, Venomoth Lv41, Forretress Lv43, Muk Lv42, Crobat Lv44
3. Bruno (Fighting): Hitmontop Lv42, Hitmonlee Lv42, Hitmonchan Lv42, Onix Lv43, Machamp Lv46
4. Karen (Dark): Umbreon Lv42, Vileplume Lv42, Gengar Lv45, Murkrow Lv44, Houndoom Lv47
Champion Lance: Gyarados Lv44, Dragonite Lv47, Dragonite Lv47, Aerodactyl Lv46, Dragonite Lv50, Charizard Lv46
After: Hall of Fame, credits
Flags set: EVENT_BEAT_ELITE_FOUR
Enables: Kanto post-game, S.S. Ticket from Elm

unstructured_notes: The E4 uses Gen 2 rules: no bag items allowed in battle but trainer can use items they have set. Koga (not Agatha) is E4 #2 in Crystal because he was moved from Fuchsia Gym. Koga's Ariados uses Spider Web + Baton Pass to trap and pass the trap to a sweeper — this is a notable AI combo. Lance has three Dragonite, which was a Gen 2 controversy since the game itself says it's illegal at that level. Hall of Fame registration captures the full party data (level, species, moves) and stores it in SRAM. After credits, Elm gives S.S. Ticket on next visit.

source_data.elite_four_strategies.md:
Will's strongest is Lv42, Lance's Dragonite is Lv50
Koga Ariados can trap with Spider Web and Baton Pass Double Team boosts. KO it fast.
Koga Forretress: Beware Explosion (huge damage, KOs Forretress too).
Bruno Hitmontop: Beware Counter — if you use physical moves, it reflects double damage.
Karen Gengar: Destiny Bond. If Gengar uses Destiny Bond and then faints, your Pokemon faints too.
Lance: No badge boost for champion battles (player has all 8 Johto badges but champion isn't a gym leader).

## 22. Post-Game: Kanto
### Elm's Lab — S.S. Ticket

unstructured_notes: Key QA: The Totodile path in rival battle 5 has Magneton at Lv34 not Lv35 — confirm this exact difference is implemented. Koga's Ariados uses Spider Web + Baton Pass combo which requires the AI to execute a move sequence correctly. Karen's Gengar with Destiny Bond is a trap: if Gengar is at low HP and uses Destiny Bond, the next hit KOs both. Hall of Fame must save all 6 party members' data. The no-bag rule during E4 battles must be enforced — only trainer items. Badge boosts (the Johto 8 badges) should apply during E4 battles since player has all 8.

goal: Implement Red battle — Mt. Silver summit, Lv81 Pikachu + full team, no dialogue, final boss mechanics

Implement post-battle sequence on player loss: whiteout to Pokemon Center, Red reappears at summit (EVENT_RED_IN_MT_SILVER not cleared on loss), player can re-challenge
Verify Red is the highest-level trainer in the game; confirm Pikachu Lv81 is higher than any gym leader or E4 member

source_data.game_progression.md:
154. Summit of Mt. Silver
155. Red says nothing — '...' — and battle begins
156. Red's Team:
    - Pikachu Lv81
    - Espeon Lv73
    - Snorlax Lv75
    - Venusaur Lv77
    - Charizard Lv77
    - Blastoise Lv77
157. Highest-level trainer in the game
158. Defeating Red triggers credits again — the true ending

unstructured_notes: Red's '......' dialogue is an iconic Crystal moment — it must be exactly 6 dots with no trainer name displayed. Red uses SPRITE_RED (the overworld player sprite from Gen 1) as his NPC sprite. His Pikachu at Lv81 makes it by far the strongest wild-equivalent in the game. Suggested movesets (from strategy context): Pikachu likely has Thunder, Thunderbolt, Quick Attack, Seismic Toss; Snorlax has Blizzard/Body Slam/Earthquake/Self-Destruct or similar. The true movesets should be read from pokecrystal-master trainer data. On loss, Red must persist at the summit for re-challenge — EVENT_RED_IN_MT_SILVER is NOT cleared on player loss. The post-victory credits are the 'true ending' of the game.

goal: Implement Team Rocket encounters — Slowpoke Well, Goldenrod Radio Tower takeover, Mahogany base, executives (Proton, Petrel, Ariana, Archer)
abstract_tasks: Implement Slowpoke Well B1F Rocket encounters: 3 Grunt battles + Executive Proton (Zubat Lv8, Koffing Lv12); set EVENT_CLEARED_SLOWPOKE_WELL after all defeated; Kurt gives Lure Ball reward

Implement Team Rocket Mahogany Base (B1F-B3F): ~10 Grunt battles, 3 Electrode Lv23 switch encounters, password puzzle ('HAIL GIOVANNI' from Murkrow), Executive encounters; Lance assists and heals party; HM06 Whirlpool reward from Lance

Implement Goldenrod Radio Tower crisis: 5 floors of Rocket Grunts, EVENT_GOLDENROD_CITY_ROCKET_TAKEOVER flag controlling overworld patrol sprites; Basement Key acquisition from Underground enables Director rescue

Implement Goldenrod Underground Rocket encounters during Radio Tower crisis: Rival Battle 4 (RIVAL1_4), Executive Proton (Zubat Lv22, Raticate Lv24, Koffing Lv22), Executive Ariana (Koffing x3 Lv30, Weezing Lv32, Koffing x2), Executive Archer (Houndour Lv33, Koffing Lv33, Houndoom Lv35) in warehouse
Implement Radio Tower Director rescue event: after Archer defeated, Director gives Clear Bell (Crystal) / sets EVENT_CLEARED_RADIO_TOWER; Clear Bell enables Tin Tower access for legendary encounters
Implement all Team Rocket Grunt trainer parties throughout all Rocket areas using ExecutiveM/ExecutiveF class data

source_data.story_events_by_map.md:
## 9. Azalea Town — Badge 2
### Slowpoke Well B1F — Team Rocket
Trigger: Enter well (Kurt rushes ahead)
Battles: 3 Rocket Grunts + Executive Proton (Zubat Lv8, Koffing Lv12)
Story: Team Rocket cutting Slowpoke tails for profit
After: Kurt gives Lure Ball, Team Rocket cleared from Azalea
Flags set: EVENT_CLEARED_SLOWPOKE_WELL

## 15. Lake of Rage + Mahogany
### Team Rocket Base (Mahogany) B1F-B3F
Trigger: Lance leads you to hidden entrance under Souvenir Shop
Battles: ~10 Rocket Grunts, 3 Electrode Lv23 (switches), Executives
Puzzle: Password doors ('HAIL GIOVANNI', spoken by Murkrow), Electrode power switches
Story: Shut down signal forcing Magikarp evolution
Lance assists: Heals your Pokemon during infiltration
Reward: HM06 Whirlpool (from Lance)
Items: TM46 Thief, Hyper Potion, Nugget, Guard Spec, Protein, X Special, Full Heal, Ice Heal, Ultra Ball
Flags set: EVENT_CLEARED_ROCKET_HIDEOUT

## 16. Goldenrod Radio Tower Crisis
### Goldenrod City — Rocket Takeover
Trigger: After Mahogany Gym, return to Goldenrod
What happens: Team Rocket has taken over Radio Tower; Rockets patrol the city
Flags: EVENT_GOLDENROD_CITY_ROCKET_TAKEOVER, EVENT_RADIO_TOWER_ROCKET_TAKEOVER

### Radio Tower 1F-5F — Clear Rocket Forces
Battles: Multiple Rocket Grunts + Executives on each floor
Story: Need Basement Key from Underground to access Director

### Goldenrod Underground — Rival Battle 4
Trigger: Encounter Silver in Underground during Radio Tower crisis
Battle: RIVAL1_4 — 5 Pokemon (~Lv30s)

### Goldenrod Underground Warehouse — Executives
Battles: Executive Proton, Executive Ariana, Executive Archer
Story: Archer is the final boss, claims to carry on Giovanni's legacy
After defeating Archer: Director rescued, Radio Tower liberated

### Radio Tower 5F — Reward
Trigger: After clearing all Rockets
Reward: CLEAR_BELL (Crystal) / RAINBOW_WING (Gold) / SILVER_WING (Silver)
Flags set: EVENT_CLEARED_RADIO_TOWER
Enables: Tin Tower access for legendary encounter (Crystal)
source_data.johto_map_scripts.md:
## Mahogany Town
### Team Rocket Base (B1F-B3F)
- B1F: ~4 Grunts, Hyper Potion, Nugget, Guard Spec, Hidden Revive
- B2F: ~3 Grunts + Executives, TM46 Thief, Hidden Full Heal, Electrode switches
- B3F: ~3 Grunts + Executives, Protein, X Special, Full Heal, Ice Heal, Ultra Ball
- Lance heals your Pokemon during infiltration
- Password puzzle ('HAIL GIOVANNI' from Murkrow)
- 3 Electrode Lv23 (can catch or KO to disable switches)
- Reward: HM06 Whirlpool from Lance

unstructured_notes: Team Rocket has 4 named executives: Proton (Slowpoke Well + Radio Tower Underground), Petrel (Radio Tower), Ariana (Radio Tower Underground), Archer (Radio Tower Underground final boss). Archer's dialogue references Giovanni and Team Rocket's legacy. The Mahogany Base password 'HAIL GIOVANNI' is obtained by listening to a Murkrow NPC — this is an interactive eavesdropping mechanic. Lance heals the player's party mid-dungeon in the Mahogany Base, which is a unique mechanic. The Electrode switches must be defeated (battle) to disable the broadcast machine — they are BATTLETYPE_WILD encounters, not trainer battles. Clear Bell (Crystal exclusive) triggers Suicune's final encounter chain at Tin Tower.
